source: Expanding Taxonomies with Implicit Edge Semantics

concepts.finding: Learns edge semantics (IS_A, PART_OF, RELATED_TO) in taxonomies from data. Edge type is predicted using embeddings of parent and child nodes. Enables automatic taxonomy expansion: given new term, predict correct parent and edge type. Achieves 85%+ accuracy on taxonomy placement.
concepts.implication: New keywords can be placed into existing intent taxonomies by predicting their parent intent and the type of relationship. Edge semantics distinguish direct intent membership from loose relatedness.
concepts.open_question: Should keyword-intent relationships be typed (IS_A, RELATED_TO, INSTANCE_OF) or is a single membership type sufficient?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Typed keyword-intent relationships (with learned edge semantics) produce more actionable keyword clustering than uniform membership assignments.
